# Let me check if I can see nearby features and plan movement toward stairs

# Find position of down stairs ">" in the map
map_lines = obs.split('--- MAP ---')[1].split('--- STATUS ---')[0].strip().split('\n')
stairs_pos = None
for y, line in enumerate(map_lines):
    if '>' in line:
        x_pos = line.index('>')
        print(f"Stairs found at relative x={x_pos}, line y={y}")
        print(f"Line content: {line}")
# Now I need to figure out which direction to move toward stairs
# The "@|" at position (48,7) - looking at map, I'm near the lower section
# Let me calculate the actual coordinates based on map layout

# Find the position of the down stairs ">" by parsing map coordinates
# In NetHack maps, positions are typically marked with x,y coordinates
map_lines = obs.split('--- MAP ---')[1].split('--- STATUS ---')[0].strip().split('\n')
print(f"Map has {len(map_lines)} lines")

# Find player position first
for y, line in enumerate(map_lines):
    if '@' in line:
        at_x = line.index('@')
        print(f"Player '@' at relative x={at_x}, line y={y}")
        print(f"Line: {line}")

# The status tells us actual position: (48, 7)
# We need to find stairs relative position and calculate the offset
for y, line in enumerate(map_lines):
    if '>' in line:
        stairs_rel_x = line.index('>')
        print(f"Stairs '>' at relative x={stairs_rel_x}, line y={y}")
        # Calculate actual stairs position based on player offset
        player_rel_x = map_lines[[i for i, l in enumerate(map_lines) if '@' in l][0]].index('@')
        x_offset = 48 - player_rel_x
        y_offset = 7 - [i for i, l in enumerate(map_lines) if '@' in l][0]
        stairs_actual_x = stairs_rel_x + x_offset
        stairs_actual_y = y + y_offset
        print(f"Calculated stairs actual position: ({stairs_actual_x}, {stairs_actual_y})")
